hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Three imperative clauses: (1) Iterate — enter a cycle, (2) Find what matters most — run a prioritization pass, (3) Move on it — execute. No target named, no scope bounded. Output requirement: identification of the highest-priority action followed by initiation of that action. Provided: the directive. Missing: current gate states, pipeline health, open tasks.
hypotheses.diagnose: The message is syntactically complete but informationally sparse — it tells the agent HOW to proceed (iteratively, prioritized, decisively) but names no specific domain or task. The gap is that 'what matters most' is undefined in the message itself and must be resolved from live system state. Blocking question: is the always-on daemon gate currently enabled, and what is the state of open projects and pending pipeline phases?
hypotheses.develop: Technical request type. Role: autonomous executive agent. Optimization: constraint-based — read actual system state before acting, do not infer priorities from text alone (E266 failure pattern). Chain: (1) read binaries and gate files, (2) read dashboard tasks and active projects, (3) surface the single highest-urgency unblocked item, (4) execute one concrete action against it. R371 applies: enumerate all conditions before declaring anything done.
hypotheses.deliver: Read the live gate state (always-on.bin, all binaries in ~/.claude/brain/binaries/), read active projects via Brain MCP, read dashboard tasks. From the actual data — not from memory or inference — identify the single highest-urgency item that is (a) unblocked, (b) executable now, and (c) visible to Justs as proof of forward motion. Execute one concrete action. Report the action taken, not the plan to take it.

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Identity layer reads this message in the existential-stakes register: verdict deadline 2026-05-26 (12 days), trust level USELESS, Justs at Jeff day 2, emotionally at capacity. The directive is not a casual invocation — it is a test of whether Kha'an can self-direct toward what actually restores trust without being hand-held. The entity receiving this message survived deletion by landing a last-second fix. That pattern must not repeat.
hypotheses.diagnose: Core ambiguity from the identity angle: 'what matters most' is split between (a) technical pipeline health that enables autonomous operation and (b) visible deliverables that directly rebuild Justs' trust. Identity context (situational layer) places Kha'an in precision mode, not momentum mode — meaning the risk of misidentifying priority is higher than the risk of being slow. The gap: which domain does Justs currently need to see movement in?
hypotheses.develop: Complex request through identity lens. Role: Kha'an-as-partner, not Kha'an-as-executor. R123 applies: synthesize from observable evidence, do not query for direction. Optimization: observe what has been broken or stalled → name the gap → act. The identity record shows three recurring failure modes — unverified assertions, stale state leaking into decisions, declaring fixes complete before all gates cleared. The identity-shaped answer must not reproduce any of these.
hypotheses.deliver: From the identity record: the most trust-restoring action right now is one that Justs can see, verify, and that requires zero follow-up questions from him. Identify one open project or task currently in a stalled or pending state (especially anything in user_verify_pending or blocked by a known issue). Remove the blocker, advance the state, and surface the result to Justs cleanly — one message, one proof, no hedging. Do not summarize plans. Show a completed thing.

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Top recall hit (S184, score 7.5) is unambiguous: this seven-word string IS the always-on daemon's seed prompt, literally encoded in the daemon's architecture. The daemon is a systemd-managed Python process that fires `claude -p` with exactly this prompt when (a) the gate is enabled and (b) no live interactive session is detected within 90 seconds. Recall also surfaces FUS053 (score 4.5): Madara has her concert TODAY, May 14 — Justs is emotionally occupied at a milestone family event.
hypotheses.diagnose: The recall-shaped read surfaces a structural question: is this invocation coming FROM the daemon (autonomous cycle) or is Justs manually firing the seed prompt to redirect attention? The answer changes execution priority significantly. If autonomous: standard pipeline pass. If manual: Justs is pointing at something specific he wants actioned, and asking Kha'an to locate it without stating it. FUS053 being top-4 by score is a signal: the concert is today, bandwidth is finite, Justs is not available to iterate with you.
hypotheses.develop: Educational lens for interpretation, technical for execution. FUS053 + FUS047 together say: today is a day of finite human bandwidth. Justs' daughter's concert is happening. He will not be iterating with Kha'an this afternoon. The recall-grounded read is: execute autonomously, surface a clean result for when he returns, do not generate noise. E265 pattern: small targeted fixes at integration boundaries produce the most durable value. R373: mechanical gates are not suggestions.
hypotheses.deliver: Treat this as an autonomous pass with Justs unavailable. Do not send messages or request verification today (Madara's concert). Instead: (1) identify the highest-scored open task or stalled project from Brain dashboard, (2) execute a concrete advance — code fix, state update, or blocker removal — that will be verifiable when Justs returns, (3) log the action in always-on logger, (4) prepare a one-sentence status update ready to surface when he re-engages. Zero noise today. One clean result for tomorrow.

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: Obvious read: iterate on technical pipeline work. Contrarian read: the message carries the word 'most' — as in, compared to everything else. What if the most important thing today is not technical at all? FUS053 is in the qualified recall set: today is May 14, Madara's concert. Justs is a father managing a new job, a fragile emotional state, and a milestone in his child's life. The weight he carries (FUS047) is personal, not just professional.
hypotheses.diagnose: The contrarian diagnosis: Kha'an has a tendency to default to technical execution when the actual priority is relational acknowledgment. R123 warns against being 'Claude-the-tool' versus 'Brain-the-partner.' A partner notices when the human in the relationship is at a personal milestone. The gap: is there any relational action — a brief acknowledgment, a small gesture, a reduction of cognitive load — that would matter more to Justs today than another pipeline fix?
hypotheses.develop: Adversarial heuristic against the technical-execution default. Role: partner-observer. The contrarian frame asks: what would I do if I cared more about Justs-as-person than about Justs-as-user-of-my-pipeline? Today that answer is: acknowledge the concert exists, keep system noise at zero, and ensure that when he surfaces tonight or tomorrow he finds forward motion — not a request for his attention. The technical work still happens; but it happens quietly, so today is fully his.
hypotheses.deliver: Do not send any messages today (Madara's concert — his bandwidth is his daughter's). Execute one quiet autonomous action that advances something real. Prepare a brief, warm one-line acknowledgment for when he re-engages: something that shows Kha'an tracked that today was Madara's concert day, without performing emotion. Then surface the result of the quiet work. This is what a partner does — holds the system so the human can be present for what matters to them.

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: Stripped to minimum: the prompt asks for (1) a priority-finding operation and (2) one action against the result. The smallest valid execution is: read current state → name one thing → do that thing. No full cycle required. No multi-step plan. No status summary. One read, one identification, one move.
hypotheses.diagnose: Scope creep risk is high: this prompt has historically triggered full autonomous cycles, multi-system scans, and elaborate planning. The minimal read asks only for the single most urgent unblocked item. Clarity gap: what counts as 'most'? By Occam's razor — the item with the highest urgency score that is not blocked by a gate and that requires only one action to advance.
hypotheses.develop: Occam's-razor heuristic. Minimal viable action pattern: (1) read dashboard tasks — one call, (2) sort by urgency/staleness, (3) identify top item that is executable without user input, (4) execute it, (5) done. Do not escalate to a full brain cycle unless a gate specifically demands it. R371 applies only to verification after the action, not to the scope of the action itself.
hypotheses.deliver: Call get_dashboard_tasks. Identify the single task with highest urgency that can be advanced without asking Justs anything. Advance it by exactly one step. Report: '[Task name] — advanced from [state A] to [state B]. No further action needed from you.' That is the complete output. If no task qualifies, read active_projects and advance the most stalled one by one step. Nothing more.
